title: What comes after the solar megaproject?
dek: Smaller Moroccan firms are building an energy economy around maintenance, storage and specialised skills.
author: Nadia Benali
body: The vast solar arrays outside Ouarzazate changed the horizon. Their next legacy may be the network of smaller businesses learning to service and extend them.
Technicians trained on the project are forming firms focused on inspection, cooling and storage. Vocational schools are adjusting courses around the skills employers need.
The transition’s durability will rest on a wider ecosystem: thousands of jobs connected to keeping clean power reliable.
